meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional mining, road, logging, ranching, and immigrant-community context.

research_grounding.title: NPS The Wintu
research_grounding.use: Clear Creek Wintu context, seasonal knowledge, acorns, salmon, burning, allotment pressure, and survival.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear
research_grounding.use: Black bear habitat, food behavior, tracks, food conflict, and practical camp hazards.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Mountain Lion
research_grounding.use: Mountain lion habitat, solitary behavior, livestock conflict, and predator sign.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the appendix page as a practical reference shelf for 1899 Jefferson State play. Do not put the final appendix text inside this JSON.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after title
opening blockquote with invented clerk note generated later
guidance section for future tables of western names, settlement tags, road tags, and work tags
guidance section for quick references about jobs, supplies, scarcity, and travel cost
guidance section for animal signs, food trouble, predator pressure, and hunting restraint
H3 Native Communities and Respectful Use
guidance section for source-aware, living-community representation
generation_guidance: Keep the appendix utilitarian and source-conscious rather than encyclopedic.
Point future writers toward quick-reference tables without filling those tables in this prompt file.
Make respectful tribal representation a standing constraint, not a decorative sidebar.
Use animals, weather, roads, names, and ledgers as table tools: sign, warning, scarcity, leverage, and travel cost.
The future page should help a facilitator improvise without flattening the setting into a generic western kit.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: Picture the appendix as the back pages of a county book: greasy thumb marks, penciled corrections, coffee shadow on the corner, and one folded map nobody trusts completely.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A good appendix should save the table from stalling while still smelling faintly of saddle soap.
must_preserve: This is a reference page, not a lore chapter.
The generated page may include future table headings, but this JSON must not contain finished table entries.
The page must include the image reference path exactly: images/appendix.jpg.
Native community guidance must be careful, grounded, and source-aware.

style_constraints.era: Everything generated from this prompt belongs to 1899: horses, ore wagons, soot-black lamps, telegraph where appropriate, hand tools, rifles, ledgers, canvas, iron, mud, pine, and courthouse paper.
style_constraints.rdr_clone: Aim at an RDR-clone western tabletop packet: moral pressure, travel friction, camp routines, hunting, law heat, debt, reputation, and open-world consequence without copying named characters, missions, towns, logos, or slogans.
style_constraints.voice: Use a weathered but practical reference voice. It should feel like a useful book owned by someone who has crossed creeks in bad weather.
style_constraints.native_representation: Native American and tribal material must be grounded, specific, and respectful: living families, trade, land rights, stewardship, survival, diplomacy, and conflict. Do not invent sacred rites, fake language, costumes, or mystical tropes.
style_constraints.shape_priority: Follow the requested section order and heading levels exactly while inventing fresh 1899 western language.

avoid: do not write finished appendix prose inside this prompt file
do not include completed reference tables, sample quotations, final animal entries, final names, or final rule text
do not mention any source packet, template packet, reference project, or inherited taxonomy
do not use copied names or terms from any prior non-western packet
do not include engine, hardware, runtime, profiling, build, deployment, or implementation details
do not include modern slang, modern technology, theme-park nostalgia, fake tribal language, or sacred ceremony invention
do not use placeholder filler such as TBD, lorem ipsum, or generic western flavor without a playable purpose
self_check: The generated markdown follows the required heading order exactly.
This JSON contains guidance only and no finished appendix prose.
The generated page helps play at the table rather than adding decorative lore.
The future page treats Native communities and animal ecology as grounded context.
No copied terms, inherited non-western vocabulary, modern props, or reference/source project names appear.
